name: BENIGN PROSTATIC HYPERTROPHY
definition: BENIGN PROSTATIC HYPERPLASIA dilatation of glandular acini in BPH

demographics.Prevalence: 50% between ages 51 + 60 years; 75-80% of all men > 80 years of age
demographics.Age: initial growth onset < 30 years of age; onset of clinical symptoms at 60 ± 9 years

dominant_finding: oval (61%) / round (22%) / pear-shaped (17%) enlargement of central gland
discriminating_features: oval (61%) / round (22%) / pear-shaped (17%) enlargement of central gland
posterior + lateral displacement of outer gland (= prostate proper) creating cleavage plane of
usually small cyst within nodules of benign prostatic hyperplasia

general: oval (61%) / round (22%) / pear-shaped (17%) enlargement of central gland
posterior + lateral displacement of outer gland (= prostate proper) creating cleavage plane of
usually small cyst within nodules of benign prostatic hyperplasia
